name: Partial compositeness
summary: SM fermions are elementary states that mix linearly with composite operators of a strongly coupled sector. Yukawa couplings emerge from the mixing strengths Lambda_L, Lambda_R rather than from fundamental Yukawas; hierarchical mixings produce hierarchical fermion masses and naturally suppress flavor-changing neutral currents.

addresses_puzzles.note: Mixing parameter Lambda_i^L * Lambda_i^R sets m_i; small mixings give light fermions naturally. RG running enhances exponential hierarchies.

touches_benchmarks.note: Generic partial compositeness saturates K-Kbar mixing bounds; alignment mechanisms (flavor symmetries within the strong sector) are required.

new_fields: composite-sector fermion partners (top partner, bottom partner, etc.)
new_symmetries: global symmetry of composite sector (often SO(5) or SU(2)_L x SU(2)_R)
introduces.note: Composite sector spectrum + 6 mixing parameters (one per fermion family/chirality).

history: Kaplan (1991) introduced partial compositeness. Bridged to extra-dimensional / RS scenarios via AdS/CFT through the 2000s; modern studies focus on top partners + flavor structure within composite-Higgs UV completions.

references.citation: Kaplan, 'Flavor at SSC energies: A new mechanism for dynamically generated fermion masses', Nucl. Phys. B 365, 259 (1991)
